ClockNodes.Name: SYSCLK_DIV_2
ClockNodes.Signpost: This divider has a fixed divisor of 2 and cannot be configured.

ClockNodes.Name: ERF0 DIV_4
ClockNodes.Signpost: This divider has a fixed divisor of 4 and cannot be configured.

ClockNodes.Name: ERF0 DIV_8
ClockNodes.Signpost: This divider has a fixed divisor of 8 and cannot be configured.

ClockNodes.Name: IBRO
ClockNodes.Signpost: To use this oscillator, please select the IBRO in one of the clock source multiplexers.

ClockNodes.Name: INRO
ClockNodes.Signpost: To use this oscillator, please select the INRO in one of the clock source multiplexers.

ClockNodes.Name: PWR SEQ
ClockNodes.Signpost: The Power Sequencer is always enabled.

ClockNodes.Name: HFXIN
ClockNodes.Signpost: The HFXIN pin should be connected to an external clock source and cannot be configured.

ClockNodes.Name: HFXOUT
ClockNodes.Signpost: The HFXOUT pin receives the output from the ERFO and cannot be configured.

ClockNodes.Name: Cortex-M4
ClockNodes.Signpost: To set up the clock for the Cortex-M4, please configure the multiplexers and dividers on the canvas.

ClockNodes.Name: IPO
ClockNodes.Signpost: To use this oscillator, please select the IPO in one of the clock source multiplexers.

ClockNodes.Name: ERFO
ClockNodes.Signpost: Use of the ERFO may be controlled by configuring the ERFO Bypass multiplexer on the canvas.

ClockNodes.Name: HART_CLK Mux
ClockNodes.Description: Mux for Highway Addressable Remote Transducer

Outputs.Description: Mux for Highway Addressable Remote Transducer

ClockNodes.Name: AFE
ClockNodes.Signpost: The processor communicates internally using the SPI0 interface to control the AFE peripherals.

ClockNodes.Name: EXT_CLK1
ClockNodes.Signpost: To configure the external clock source on P0.12, the MISC EXT_CLK1 signal must be assigned using the Pin Config tool.

ClockNodes.Name: EXT_CLK2
ClockNodes.Signpost: To configure the external clock source on P0.12, the MISC EXT_CLK2 signal must be assigned using the Pin Config tool.

ClockNodes.Name: SPI0
ClockNodes.Signpost: SPI0 is internally connected and used to communicate with the Analog Front-End (AFE).

Fields.Name: PEND
Fields.Documentation: When Flash operation is in progress (busy), Flash reads and writes will fail. When PEND is set, write to all Flash registers, with exception of the Flash interrupt register, are ignored.

Enum.Name: IDLE
Enum.Documentation: Idle.

Enum.Name: BUSY
Enum.Documentation: Busy.

Fields.Name: UNLOCK
Fields.Documentation: The correct unlock code must be written to these four bits before any Flash write or erase operation is allowed.

Enum.Name: UNLOCKED
Enum.Documentation: Flash Unlocked.

Enum.Name: LOCKED
Enum.Documentation: Flash Locked.

Fields.Name: DONE
Fields.Documentation: This bit is set to 1 upon Flash write or erase completion.

Enum.Name: INACTIVE
Enum.Documentation: No Interrupt is Pending.

Enum.Name: PENDING
Enum.Documentation: An Interrupt is Pending.

Fields.Name: AF
Fields.Documentation: This bit is set when an attempt is made to write the flash while the flash is busy or the flash is locked. This bit can only be set to 1 by hardware.

Fields.Name: GPIO_WE
Fields.Documentation: This bit enables all GPIO pins as potential wakeup sources. Any GPIO configured for wakeup is capable of causing an exit from IDLE or STANDBY modes when this bit is set.

Enum.Documentation: Wake up Disable.

Enum.Name: EN
Enum.Documentation: Wake up Enable.

Fields.Name: LPTMR1_WE

Fields.Name: LPUART0_WE

Fields.Name: ERFO_PD
Fields.Documentation: This bit selects 32MHz Crystal power oscillator (ERFO) state in DEEPSLEEP mode. This field must be set to 1 before entering DEEPSLEEP.

Enum.Name: ACTIVE
Enum.Documentation: Mode is Active.

Enum.Name: DEEPSLEEP
Enum.Documentation: Powered Down in DEEPSLEEP.

Fields.Name: IPO_PD
Fields.Documentation: This bit selects IPO clock power state in DEEPSLEEP mode.

Fields.Name: IBRO_PD
Fields.Documentation: This bit selects 7.3728 MHz clock power state in DEEPSLEEP mode.

Enum.Name: ACTIVE
Enum.Documentation: Mode is Active.

Enum.Name: DEEPSLEEP
Enum.Documentation: Powered Down in DEEPSLEEP.

Fields.Name: ERFO_BP
Fields.Documentation: This bit is set to 0 on a POR is not affected by other forms of reset. Set this field to 1 to use a square wave input as the clock source for the ERFO driving the HFXIN pin.

Registers.Documentation: Each bit controls the GPIO_EN setting for one GPIO pin on the associated port. These bits configure the corresponding device pin for digital I/O or an alternate function mode. The fields can be modified directly by writing to this register or indirectly through GPIOn_EN0_SET or GPIOn_EN0_CLR.

Registers.Documentation: Each bit controls the GPIO_EN setting for one GPIO pin on the associated port. These bits configure the corresponding device pin for digital I/O or an alternate function mode. The fields can be modified directly by writing to this register or indirectly through GPIOn_EN0_SET or GPIOn_EN0_CLR.

Fields.Documentation: Controls whether the cache is bypassed or is in use. Changing the state of this bit will cause the instruction cache to be flushed and its contents invalidated.

Enum.Name: DIS
Enum.Documentation: Instruction data is stored in the line fill buffer but is not written to main cache memory array.

Enum.Name: EN
Enum.Documentation: Cache Enabled.

Fields.Name: RDY
Fields.Documentation: Cleared by hardware when at any time the cache as a whole is invalidated (including a system reset). When this bit is 0, the cache is effectively in bypass mode (instruction fetches will come from main memory or from the line fill buffer). Set by hardware when the invalidate operation is complete and the cache is ready.
